opening_phrases: Good morning, please have a seat and make yourself comfortable.
Good afternoon. I'm Officer {name} and I'll be conducting your naturalization interview today.
Welcome. Before we begin, do you have any questions about today's process?
Please raise your right hand. Do you swear to tell the truth during this interview?
Let's start by verifying some basic information from your application.
I have your N-400 application here. Let me just review a few things with you.
identity_verification: Can you please state your full legal name as it appears on your application?
Please spell your last name for me.
Can you show me your green card and a government-issued ID?
I see your name is {name}. Is that correct?
How long have you had your current address?
eligibility_questions: I see here that you're applying based on 5 years as a permanent resident. Is that correct?
When did you become a permanent resident?
You've been married to {spouse} since {date}. Is your spouse a U.S. citizen?
Tell me about the basis for your application for naturalization.
Have you lived in this state for at least 3 months?
Are you still married to the same U.S. citizen mentioned in your application?
background_questions: Let's talk about your travels outside the United States.
I see here that you took a trip to {country} for {days} days. Tell me about that trip.
What was the purpose of your travel to {country}?
Did you maintain your residence in the United States during that time?
Let's go through your employment history.
Tell me about your employment during the past 5 years.
problem_indicators: I notice here that you were outside the United States for {days} days. That's quite a long time.
Can you explain this gap in your employment history?
I see that you indicated some issues with tax filing. Can you tell me about that?
Your application shows that you were arrested in {year}. I need you to tell me about that incident.
You mentioned that you didn't register for Selective Service. Can you explain why?
I need to understand this travel pattern better.
civics_introduction: Now we're going to move on to the civics portion of the test.
I'm going to ask you some questions about U.S. history and government.
These questions are from the official study materials you should have received.
I'll ask you up to 10 questions, and you need to answer 6 correctly to pass.
Answer with what you studied - give me the official answer.
If you don't know an answer, that's okay, just say 'I don't know.'
english_test_phrases: Now let's test your English ability.
I'm going to give you something to read. Please read it out loud.
Here's a sentence for you to write. Write it exactly as I say it.
We've been testing your English speaking during our conversation.
oath_discussion: Let's talk about the Oath of Allegiance.
Are you willing to take the full Oath of Allegiance to the United States?
Do you understand what the oath means?
Are you willing to bear arms on behalf of the United States when required by law?
Are you willing to perform noncombat services for the U.S. when required by law?
Will you support and defend the Constitution of the United States?
closing_statements: Based on our interview today, I'm going to recommend your application for approval.
Congratulations! You've passed your naturalization interview.
I need to review some additional documents before I can make a decision.
You'll receive a letter in the mail with our decision within 30 days.
Your oath ceremony will be scheduled, and you'll receive information by mail.
Do you have any questions about what happens next?

long_trips: You were outside the U.S. for {days} days. Did you get a re-entry permit?
How did you maintain your ties to the United States during this time?
Did you file U.S. taxes while you were abroad?
What documentation do you have about this trip?
Was this trip planned or an emergency?
frequent_travel: I see you travel to {country} frequently. What's the reason for these trips?
Do you have family or business interests in {country}?
Are you planning to continue this travel pattern after naturalization?

tax_issues: You indicated that you didn't file taxes in {year}. What happened?
Do you have documentation showing you've resolved this with the IRS?
Have you filed all required tax returns since then?
Do you currently owe any taxes to the IRS?
Can you provide proof of tax compliance?
criminal_history: Tell me exactly what happened that led to your arrest.
How was this resolved in court?
Do you have the court disposition documents with you?
Have you had any other contact with law enforcement?
Have you completed all requirements of your sentence?
selective_service: Why didn't you register for Selective Service when you turned 18?
Were you aware that you were required to register?
Do you have a Status Information Letter from Selective Service?
This could affect your naturalization. Do you understand that?

reading_sentences: America is the land of freedom.
All people want to be free.
America is the home of the brave.
Citizens have the right to vote.
Democracy is a government of the people.
The flag has stars and stripes.
I want to be an American citizen.
The Constitution is the supreme law.
We the People formed this country.
writing_sentences: Citizens have the right to vote.
America is the land of the free.
All people want to be free.
America is the home of the brave.
We live in the United States.
I want to be a citizen.
Democracy is the government of the people.

approved: Congratulations! Based on our interview today and your application, I'm pleased to inform you that your naturalization application has been approved.
You've successfully completed all requirements for U.S. citizenship. Your application is approved.
I'm happy to tell you that you've passed your naturalization interview and test. Congratulations!
continued: I need to review some additional documentation before I can make a final decision on your case.
Your case requires additional review. You'll receive a letter within 30 days with our decision.
I need you to provide some additional documents. You'll receive a list of what we need.
denied: I'm sorry, but based on our interview today, I cannot approve your application at this time.
There are some issues with your case that prevent approval. You'll receive a detailed explanation by mail.
Your application cannot be approved today. You have the right to appeal this decision.

common_mistakes_officers_address: I need the official answer, not your own words.
That's close, but I need the specific answer from your study materials.
Let me repeat the question for you.
Take your time to think about it.
That's not quite right. Do you know the official answer?
I can see you're nervous. Just relax and give me the answer you studied.
Remember, I need the answer exactly as it appears in the civics study guide.
